_meta.name: Beghou Arc — Color Tokens
_meta.note: Stopgap token file derived from the non-Kendo design system spec. Use until the Kendo ThemeBuilder export is ready, then replace with the ThemeBuilder variables.json. This file is library-agnostic — values can be mapped to Kendo Sass variables, CSS custom properties, or any other theme system.

navy.usage: Primary brand color. Used for primary buttons, active nav, body text on light surfaces, branded accents.

navyHover.usage: Hover state for primary actions. The only 'hover variant' that exists in the system.

onPrimary.usage: Text and icons on navy backgrounds.

magenta.usage: BRAND ACCENT ONLY. Used in the Beghou logo's spark glyph and rarely in marketing surfaces (~5% of the visual field). NEVER use on UI — not for secondary buttons, links, interactive states, focus rings, or chart series. If a prototype needs a secondary action, it's an outlined navy button on transparent — never magenta.

app.usage: Main page background. The canvas everything sits on.

card.usage: Default card background — soft blue-tinted. Arc's signature surface for cards, dialog titlebars, and tinted regions.

soft.usage: Very light blue for subtle panels, notification banners, inactive states.

50.usage: Lightest neutral. Subtle row hovers, alternating grid rows.

100.usage: Subtle backgrounds, muted surfaces, ChipList chip background, Solid Base chip fill.

200.usage: Borders, disabled backgrounds, dividers, ChipList chip hover/selected.

900.usage: Dark text and icons (same value as brand.navy — aliased here for neutral-scale references).

formInput.usage: Form input borders — Textbox, NumericTextbox, TextArea, DropDownList, ComboBox, AutoComplete, Multiselect, DropDownTree, DateInput, Checkbox (unchecked), RadioButton (unselected), Switch (off). 50% navy — darker than container borders so inputs read clearly on tinted surfaces.

container.usage: Card borders, dividers, separators, Dialog footer top borders, row separators. 8% navy — lighter than input borders so containers recede behind their contents.

emphasized.usage: Stronger border for emphasized separators and specific focus-related treatments. 16% navy. Not interchangeable with formInput or container.

success.usage: Positive status — Active, Approved, Complete. Text on success fill is white.

warning.usage: Caution status — Pending Review, Attention Needed. Text on warning fill is NAVY (#020434), not white — white-on-orange fails WCAG AAA contrast.

error.usage: Failure status — Failed, Rejected, Expired. Text on error fill is white.

info.usage: Informational status — New, Updated, In Progress. Text on info fill is white. NOTE: when an Info MessageBox sits on a #EDF0FA Card surface, the blue contrast is weak — use brand.navy bg with white text (the MessageBox 'Primary' variant) instead.

focusRing.usage: 2px navy at 30% opacity, 2px offset. Standard focus ring across all interactive elements.

chart._note: Charts only — never use these for UI elements (buttons, backgrounds, chrome). Default rotation: positions 1–5. Named accents for emphasis series.

series.name: Beghou Navy
series._note: Available but causes confusion when UI also uses navy heavily — prefer other positions

_rulesOfThumb.primary: Always navy. Never black, never blue.
_rulesOfThumb.magenta: Logo only. Not for any UI surface — buttons, icons, hover, focus, chart series. If in doubt, leave magenta out.
_rulesOfThumb.cards: #EDF0FA card surface is Arc's signature. Don't use white-on-white card stacking.
_rulesOfThumb.borders: Form inputs at 50% navy, containers at 8% navy. Never solid grey.
_rulesOfThumb.formInputFill: Always explicit #FFFFFF. Never transparent — inputs must be visible against tinted Card Surfaces and alternating grid rows.
_rulesOfThumb.semanticUsage: Each semantic color has ONE shade. For pale backgrounds, use the solid color at low opacity (e.g., rgba(255,162,2,0.12) for a warning banner).
